api/client/javascript/src/portal.purpose: Restricted portal-scoped JavaScript client for end-customer access. Exposes a single OpenMeter class authenticated via a required portalToken that provides only the portal meter-query endpoint — intentionally a much smaller surface than the full admin client.

patterns.name: Reuse utilities from the parent client
patterns.description: All shared logic (RequestOptions, paths/operations types, transformResponse, encodeDates) is imported from ../client/common.js, ../client/schemas.js, and ../client/utils.js. This file adds only portal-specific wiring.
patterns.example:
import type { operations, paths } from '../client/schemas.js'
import { encodeDates, transformResponse } from '../client/utils.js'

patterns.name: portalToken replaces apiKey and is required
patterns.description: Config requires portalToken: string (not optional). The Authorization header is always `Bearer ${config.portalToken}`. The querySerializer (array form/explode, object deepObject/explode) must stay in sync with the main client.

patterns.name: Explicit Accept: application/json on the meter query
patterns.description: The portal meter-query endpoint returns CSV when no Accept header is set. Always pass Accept: application/json and cast the result to the exact operations response type.
patterns.example: const resp = await this.client.GET('/api/v1/portal/meters/{meterSlug}/query', { headers: { Accept: 'application/json' }, ... }); return transformResponse(resp) as operations['queryPortalMeter']['responses']['200']['content']['application/json']

key_file_guides.role: The entire portal SDK: creates an openapi-fetch client, wires Bearer portalToken and the querySerializer, exposes a single query() method for /api/v1/portal/meters/{meterSlug}/query.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must pass Accept: application/json explicitly or the endpoint returns CSV. querySerializer shape must stay in sync with the main client's index.ts.

anti_patterns: Adding admin-level endpoints to the portal client — it is intentionally restricted to portal-scoped access.
Redefining transformResponse or encodeDates instead of importing from ../client/utils.js.
Making portalToken optional in Config — portal requests always require authentication.
Omitting Accept: application/json on the meter query — the endpoint falls back to CSV.

decisions.decision: The portal client is a separate minimal class, not a subset configuration of the main OpenMeter client.
decisions.rationale: Prevents accidentally exposing admin endpoints to end-customer portal tokens and makes the restricted surface explicit at the type level.

code_examples.scenario: Querying a portal meter with the required Accept header

api/client/javascript/src/react.purpose: Thin React integration providing a context/provider/hook trio (OpenMeterContext, OpenMeterProvider, useOpenMeter) so React apps can inject and consume a portal OpenMeter client anywhere in the tree. Single file; all SDK logic lives in ../portal/.

patterns.name: Re-export the portal surface wholesale
patterns.description: context.tsx does `export * from '../portal/index.js'` so all portal SDK types/functions are available from the react import path. New exports should come via re-export, not local definitions.

patterns.name: Null-initialized context with undefined-throws hook
patterns.description: OpenMeterContext defaults to null (unauthenticated). useOpenMeter throws only when the context is undefined (used outside a provider); callers handle null themselves.
patterns.example:
const ctx = createContext<OpenMeter | null>(null)
if (typeof context === 'undefined') { throw new Error('useOpenMeter must be used within a OpenMeterProvider') }

patterns.name: 'use client' directive at top
patterns.description: The file is marked 'use client' for Next.js App Router compatibility. Any change must preserve this directive as the very first line.

key_file_guides.role: Entire folder. Exports OpenMeterContext, OpenMeterProvider, useOpenMeter and re-exports all portal SDK symbols.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No business logic or API methods here — all SDK behaviour belongs in ../portal/. New exports via re-export, not local definitions. Do not remove 'use client'.

anti_patterns: Defining SDK methods or API call logic in context.tsx instead of delegating to ../portal/.
Removing the 'use client' directive — breaks Next.js App Router.
Creating a second context/provider for different concerns — extend the OpenMeter type in portal/ instead.
Throwing inside useOpenMeter when the value is null — null is a valid unauthenticated state, only undefined should throw.

decisions.decision: Context default is null, not a noop object.
decisions.rationale: Forces callers to handle the unauthenticated state explicitly rather than silently calling noop methods that appear to work.

decisions.decision: All portal exports are re-exported from this entry point.
decisions.rationale: Consumers importing from the react path get the full portal SDK without a second import, while ../portal/ remains the single source of truth.

code_examples.scenario: Wrap the app with the provider and consume the client

api/client/javascript/src/zod.purpose: Auto-generated Zod validation schemas for all OpenMeter API query parameters and request bodies, produced by orval from the OpenAPI spec. Exports typed Zod objects plus per-operation constant defaults/regex patterns — never hand-edit.

patterns.name: Generated by orval — do not edit
patterns.description: index.ts carries the 'Generated by orval v8.7.0 / Do not edit manually.' header. All schemas are regenerated from the OpenAPI spec; manual edits are overwritten on the next make gen-api.
patterns.example: /** Generated by orval v8.7.0 🍺 Do not edit manually. */

patterns.name: Constants exported before each schema
patterns.description: Each operation exports named constants for defaults, min/max, and RegExp patterns before its zod.object() (e.g. listAddonsQueryPageDefault = 1, listAddonsQueryKeyItemRegExp), referenced inside the schema.
patterns.example:
export const listAddonsQueryPageSizeMax = 1000
export const ListAddonsQueryParams = zod.object({ pageSize: zod.coerce.number().min(1).max(listAddonsQueryPageSizeMax)... })

patterns.name: zod.coerce for all scalar fields
patterns.description: Query parameters use zod.coerce.string()/number()/boolean() to handle URL string coercion, matching how form-encoded query strings arrive.

patterns.name: Naming convention per operation
patterns.description: Schema objects are named <OperationPascalCase>QueryParams or <OperationPascalCase>Body (e.g. ListAddonsQueryParams, CreateAddonBody).
patterns.example: export const ListAddonsQueryParams = zod.object({ ... }); export const CreateAddonBody = zod.object({ ... })

key_file_guides.role: Single generated file exporting all Zod schemas for query-param and body validation, plus the per-operation constants (defaults, regexps, min/max).
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated by orval on make gen-api. Import these schemas for server-side validation of incoming params.

anti_patterns: Manually editing index.ts — it is regenerated by orval from the OpenAPI spec on make gen-api.
Redefining Zod schemas locally when the generated schemas already cover the operation.
Using the raw generated constants (defaults/regexps) in application logic — they are schema implementation details subject to regeneration.

decisions.decision: Zod schemas are generated by orval from the same OpenAPI spec that generates the TypeScript types.
decisions.rationale: Keeps runtime validation (Zod) and compile-time types (TypeScript) in sync with the TypeSpec source of truth — a single make gen-api updates both.

code_examples.scenario: Using a generated query-param schema for server-side validation
code_examples.code:
import { ListAddonsQueryParams } from '@openmeter/sdk/zod'
const parsed = ListAddonsQueryParams.parse(req.query)
